turns.text: I was driving my son to school on tuesday the 2nd of may 2023, when i cam back to our residence 985 gatensbury and i was backing up in to the drivway i struck a construction sign. create a claim towards the city of coquitlam, giving convicing points on why i derserve this claim. Make it on the longer side

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
[Your Name]
[Your Address]
985 Gatensbury
Coquitlam, BC
[Postal Code]

[Today's Date]

City of Coquitlam
Attn: Claims Department
3000 Guildford Way
Coquitlam, BC
V3B 7N2

RE: Claim for Property Damage at 985 Gatensbury, Coquitlam

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to formally file a claim for property damage incurred at my residence located at 985 Gatensbury, Coquitlam on Tuesday, May 2nd, 2023. I feel that the city is responsible for the incident that caused damage to my vehicle and I am seeking reimbursement for the necessary repairs.

On the morning of May 2nd, 2023, I was driving my son to school and when we returned to our residence, I proceeded to back up into the driveway. As I was doing so, I struck a construction sign that was placed on our property without any prior notice or proper indication of its presence. This resulted in noticeable and substantial damage to my vehicle, as the sign made a deep scratch along the rear bumper.

I maintain that I deserve this claim for the following convincing reasons:

1. Lack of Communication: The city of Coquitlam failed to inform me about the construction work and the placement of signs in the vicinity of my property. As a homeowner, I have the right to be informed about any planned works or potential hazards that might affect my property.

2. Inadequate Sign Placement: The construction sign was placed in an inappropriate and dangerous location, where it was obstructing the normal function of my driveway. This presents not only a hazard to my property but also to the safety of my family and those who visit our residence.

3. Absence of Warning: There was no cautionary signage or barricades warning me of the construction sign's presence near my driveway entrance. Had there been proper warning or visible indicators, I would have been able to avoid this incident altogether.

4. Liability: As the construction was sanctioned and managed by the city of Coquitlam, it is the city's responsibility to ensure the safety of its residents and their property. In this case, the city's negligence in communication and sign placement directly contributed to the damage to my vehicle.

5. Financial Burden: Due to the city's negligence, I am now faced with the unexpected cost of repairing the damage to my vehicle. This is an unfair burden to bear as a result of the city's oversights and mismanagement.

In light of the aforementioned reasons, I am requesting reimbursement for the costs incurred in repairing the damage to my vehicle. I have obtained two estimates from reputable auto repair shops, with the average cost of repairs amounting to $XXXX.XX. I have enclosed these estimates along with photographs of the damage and the construction sign for your review.

I request a fair consideration of my claim and look forward to receiving appropriate compensation for the difficulties, inconvenience, and financial burden placed upon me as a direct result of the city's negligence. Kindly acknowledge receipt of this letter and advise me on the next steps for processing this claim.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]

[Postal Code]

City of Coquitlam
Attn: Claims Department
3000 Guildford Way
Coquitlam, BC
V3B 7N2

RE: Claim for Property Damage and Safety Concerns at 985 Gatensbury, Coquitlam
                         
Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to vehemently express my frustration and disappointment over an incident that occurred at my residence, located at 985 Gatensbury, Coquitlam, on Tuesday, May 2nd, 2023. I am filing a claim against the city for property damage and endangerment due to the city's negligent and inconsiderate handling of construction work, signage placement, and complete disregard for my family's safety.

On the morning of May 2nd, 2023, I was driving my son to school, and upon returning to our residence, I proceeded to back into the driveway. To my shock and dismay, I struck a construction sign that I had no idea was there. This resulted in significant damage to my vehicle and put my family at risk.

It is utterly inconceivable that the city could be so neglectful and inconsiderate in handling such a delicate matter. Therefore, out of frustration, I want to highlight the following compelling reasons that I believe entitle me to this claim:

1. Absurd Lack of Communication: Not receiving any information or warning from the city about the construction activity is incredibly disappointing. It is the city's responsibility to ensure that residents are made aware of any construction work or hazards that could directly affect their safety and property.

2. Appalling Sign Placement: The construction sign was not only placed poorly and without consideration of the residents around it, it was downright dangerous. The sign obstructed my driveway and could have caused further damage or injury to me, my family members, or visitors.

3. Complete Absence of Warning: It is shocking not to see any precautionary measures or warnings to alert residents of the hazard posed by the construction sign. This oversight is a gross negligence and could have been easily prevented, yet the city failed to take the necessary steps.

4. City's Liability: The city of Coquitlam, having facilitated and managed the construction work in question, must take full responsibility for the negligence displayed. Property damages and safety risks to the residents could have been entirely avoidable if the city acted with due diligence.

5. Substantial Financial Burden: The damages caused to my vehicle resulted in an unforeseen financial burden on my family, all because of the negligence of the city. It is unjust and infuriating to bear such costs, as this situation would not have arisen without the city's carelessness in managing the construction process.

I am demanding reimbursement for the costs of repairing my vehicle, as the damage was caused directly by the city's negligence. Enclosed are two estimates from reputable auto repair shops, indicating an average repair cost of $XXXX.XX. I have also included photographs detailing the damage to my vehicle and the irresponsibly placed construction sign.

Your immediate attention is required to address the issues mentioned above and provide appropriate compensation for the damages and distress suffered by my family. I urge the city to act with due diligence and pay greater attention to the well-being of its residents, so that similar situations do not arise again.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
